description: Styled wrappers around native table elements. Table renders a <table> wrapped in a scroll container; TableHeader / TableBody / TableFooter are <thead> / <tbody> / <tfoot>; TableHead is <th>; TableCell is <td>; TableRow is <tr>; TableCaption is <caption>. All native table semantics apply, so screen readers can navigate cell-by-cell with AT-specific keystrokes.

focus.notes: The table itself is not focusable. Interactive content inside cells (buttons, links, checkboxes) retains its own focus behavior.

announcements: Announces as a table with its row / column count.
Each TableHead (<th>) is used as a column header reference when cells are navigated.
TableCaption (if present) is read at the start of the table.
screenReader.notes: TableHead does not set scope="col" by default; for complex tables (multi-level headers, row headers) add scope / headers / id attributes yourself. Simple tables work fine with the defaults.

contrast.text: Header text uses text-muted-foreground; cell text uses foreground. Both meet WCAG AA on bg-background
contrast.ui: Row borders use border token; selected / hover states use bg-muted/50. All meet AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Only a color transition on row hover; no motion-based animations

consumerResponsibilities: Use a TableCaption or aria-label / aria-labelledby to name the table; unnamed tables are hard to navigate for screen reader users.
Set scope="col" on TableHead (or scope="row" on row headers) for tables with complex header relationships.
Don't use <Table> for layout; it implies a data grid to assistive tech. Use <div> + CSS grid for visual layouts.
For sortable / selectable tables, wire aria-sort on the relevant TableHead and aria-selected on rows yourself; the primitives don't add these automatically.
